design_philosophy.core_loop: Conversation, inventory-based puzzle solving, and environmental exploration to progress through narrative scenes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Solving character-driven mysteries and moral conflicts within a predetermined narrative framework
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a cynical travel agent (grim reaper) navigating a film noir-inflected afterlife, unraveling corruption and betrayal
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses death/failure states entirely—player cannot lose or reach unwinnable conditions. Prioritizes narrative and character development over challenge. Blends Aztec mythology with 1950s film noir aesthetics as deliberate thematic choice. Emphasizes diegetic interaction (Manny turns head toward objects) over UI abstractions.

difficulty.lives_system: No lives system; player cannot die
difficulty.continues_system: No continues needed; game always progresses
difficulty.checkpointing: Scene-based progression; puzzles may require conversation hints but never block completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player has no difficulty settings. Puzzles are solvable through conversation trees that provide hints. No time pressure or resource scarcity creates challenge.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Collecting objects and solving environmental puzzles unlocks dialogue options and narrative advancement. Characters gate progression through puzzle solutions and story beats.
replayability_hooks: Multiple dialogue tree branches reveal character depth
Film noir narrative encourages second playthrough for thematic appreciation

world_building_techniques: Pre-rendered 3D backgrounds establish retro-futuristic 1950s Land of the Dead aesthetic
Dialogue trees with conversation partners provide lore exposition and character backstory
Pre-rendered 3D cutscenes advance plot and emotional beats
Diegetic animations (Manny pulling items from jacket, turning head) reinforce world immersion
Spatial design implies bureaucratic infrastructure (Department of Death, travel packages)

faction_indicators: Department of Death (bureaucratic institution determining travel package merit based on life deeds)
Demonic underlings serving maintenance and service roles
Travel agent class (Manny's profession; implies social hierarchy)
danger_signals: Sproutella-filled darts causing 'death-within-death' via reincarnation—suggests enforced consequences for transgressions
Souls who reject the Ninth Underworld and settle in Land of Dead—implies those who lose faith become permanent underclass
Four-year journey on foot vs. express train based on lifetime morality—systemic reward/punishment
environmental_storytelling: Retro-futuristic 1950s infrastructure (sports cars, luxury ocean cruises) contrasts with skeletal calaca appearance
Day of the Dead annual visitation rights establish cyclical connection between living and dead
Department of Death bureaucracy embodies afterlife as institutionalized system rather than spiritual transcendence
